findings.title: New `/clear` coverage misses the `needs_context` state transitions changed in this diff
findings.description: The behavior change is not limited to `build_context_content()`: this patch also changes the `needs_context` bookkeeping in both `handle_message()` and `execute_in_session()`. The added tests only verify the pure string-building helper, so a regression in either send path (for example, `/clear` not setting or preserving `needs_context` after queue/direct send) would still pass. Because `/clear` now relies on those stateful branches to behave like `/new`, the current test set does not cover the riskiest part of the change.
findings.suggestion: Add tests around the state transitions, not just the content helper. The cleanest option is to extract the `/new`|`/clear` classification and `needs_context` transition logic into a small pure helper and unit-test queued/direct paths for both commands, including case-insensitive inputs.

summary: No obvious correctness bug in the diff itself, but the new `/clear` behavior is only partially tested; the stateful branches changed by the patch still lack regression coverage.
